arts.ttl: Stoke City v Middlesbrough: How to watch, live stream, TV channel, kick-off time
arts.sum: Stoke fans will tell you that a trip to the bet365 Stadium is the ideal cure for the malaise Boro are suffering right now, but they will also know that back-to-back home games - Watford are in the Potteries on Saturday - offer their own team a way clear of the drop zone. Stoke City will be looking to claim back-to-back home wins for the first time this season when they host Michael Carrick's Middlesbrough in the Championship on Tuesday.

arts.ttl: Boro crossroads week for Michael Carrick starts with Stoke City trip
arts.sum: The players as a whole lacked a spring in their step at Ashton Gate which goes hand in hand with a team which is not doing well on the pitch. In addition, despite the defensive performance on Friday, Carrick had no less than FOUR attack-minded players on the pitch.

arts.ttl: Michael Carrick saying all the right things as Middlesbrough to go Stoke City but dismisses past
arts.sum: The defeat means Boro are now on a five-match losing streak - Stoke fans will tell you that a trip to the bet365 Stadium is the ideal cure for such a malaise - and Carrick insists his team are facing another tough game, despite the hosts’ struggles at the wrong end of the Championship table. Referring to new Stoke boss Mark Robins, Carrick dismissed past Coventry v Boro clashes, instead telling TeessideLive : “He's only gone in fairly recently (from Coventry City) and had a mixture of results - but some really good ones.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough's biggest area to improve is also Michael Carrick's biggest source for belief
arts.sum: Carrick explained: “I think there's been a lot of times where we've been in good positions in games, really, and finishing games off and managing games towards the second half is something we need to improve on. But I think, for us as a group, the thing that shows us and gives us that hope and more belief than hope actually, is the amount of times we’ve been up in games and in good positions in games.

arts.ttl: The Middlesbrough player who created many of Brian Clough's goals but only after catching the bus
arts.sum: In a time when Boro regularly played a 2-3-5 formation, the pair were part of the ’£50 forward line’, which as well as Clough and McLean, also featured Peacock, Billie Day and Eddie Holliday. Known as Derek throughout his Boro career, McLean would come through the Boro youth ranks and into the first team at the same time as Brian Clough and Alan Peacock.

arts.ttl: Michael Carrick's transfer 'click' hope and Kelechi Iheanacho Middlesbrough role admission
arts.sum: The boys that have come in are all good players, and they’ve fitted into the group fantastically well. He continued: “There’s no getting away from the fact that it’s been a difficult time for those players to come into the team and the group.
